title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Speed Booster Test

questions.question: The base of a right pyramid is an equilateral triangle of side 10√3 cm. If the total surface area of the pyramid is 270√3 sq. cm, its height is ?
questions.explanation: Given: The base of the pyramid is an equilateral triangle with side 10310\sqrt{3}103​​​ cm. The total surface area of the pyramid is 2703 270\sqrt{3}2703​​ sq. cm. We need to find the height of the pyramid. Concept Used: The total surface area of a pyramid is the sum of the base area and the lateral surface area. For a pyramid with an equilateral triangle base: Base area of an equilateral triangle is: ​Abase=34×side2A_{\text{base}} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times \text{side}^2Abase​=43​​×side2​​ Total surface area of a pyramid: ​Atotal=Abase+AlateralA_{\text{total}} = A_{\text{base}} + A_{\text{lateral}}Atotal​=Abase​+Alateral​​​ The lateral surface area depends on the slant height and the perimeter of the base. Area of an equilateral triangle: ​Abase=34×side2A_{\text{base}} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times \text{side}^2Abase​=43​​×side2​​ ​Atotal=Abase+AlateralA_{\text{total}} = A_{\text{base}} + A_{\text{lateral}}Atotal​=Abase​+Alateral​​​ Pythagoras theorem for the height of the pyramid: ​h2+(inradius)2=slant height2h^2 + \left( inradius \right)^2 = \text{slant height}^2h2+(inradius)2=slant height2​​ Solution: Area of Base = 34×(103)2\frac{\sqrt3}4 × (10\sqrt3) ^ 243​​×(103​)2​​ = 75375\sqrt3753​​ cm2 Lateral surface area = 2703−753 270\sqrt3 - 75\sqrt32703​−753​​ = 1953 195\sqrt31953​​ cm2 Area of each lateral surface = 19533\frac{195\sqrt3}331953​​​ = 65365\sqrt3 653​ cm2 => 12×103×h=653\frac12 × 10\sqrt3 × h = 65\sqrt321​×103​×h=653​​​ => h = 13 cm (slant height) Inradius = side of triangle23=10323=5\frac{side\ of\ triangle}{2\sqrt3} = \frac{10\sqrt3}{2\sqrt3} = 523​side of triangle​=23​103​​=5 Now, height of pyramid = 132−52\sqrt{13 ^ 2 - 5 ^ 2}132−52​​ = 12 cm ​

questions.question: A can finish a piece of work in 36 days and B in 72 days. They begin the work together, but A leaves 6 days before the completion of the work. In how many total days the complete work will be done?
questions.explanation: Given: Time taken by A = 36 days Time taken by B = 72 days A leaves 6 days before the completion of the work. Formula Used: Work Done = Rate of Work × Time Solution: Let the total number of days taken to complete the work be x. Since A leaves 6 days before completion, A works for (x - 6) days. B works for the entire duration of x days. Formulate the work equation: ​x−636+x72=1\frac{x - 6}{36} + \frac{x}{72} = 136x−6​+72x​=1​​ 2(x - 6) + x = 72 2x - 12 + x = 72 3x = 84 x = 28 The complete work will be done in 28 days. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d) Exam Hall Method: ​

questions.question: A certain sum amounts to ₹4,620 after two years at a certain rate of simple interest and to ₹4,725 after two and a half years at the same rate. Find the annual percentage rate.
questions.explanation: Given: Amount after 2 years A1 A_1 A1​​= ₹4,620 Amount after 2.5 years A2A_2 A2​​= ₹4,725 Time difference = 0.5 years Formula Used: Simple Interest formula: ​A=P+SI=P+P×r×t100A = P + \text{SI} = P + \frac{P \times r \times t}{100}A=P+SI=P+100P×r×t​​​ where P = principal, r = annual rate, t = time in years. Solution: Let P be the principal and r be the annual rate. For 2 years: ​4620=P+P×r×21004620 = P + \frac{P \times r \times 2}{100}4620=P+100P×r×2​​​ For 2.5 years: ​4725=P+P×r×2.51004725 = P + \frac{P \times r \times 2.5}{100}4725=P+100P×r×2.5​​​ Now, ​4725−4620=P×r×2.5100−P×r×2100 105=P×r×0.5100 P×r=105×1000.5=210004725 - 4620 = \frac{P \times r \times 2.5}{100} - \frac{P \times r \times 2}{100}\\ \ \\105 = \frac{P \times r \times 0.5}{100}\\ \ \\P \times r = \frac{105 \times 100}{0.5} = 210004725−4620=100P×r×2.5​−100P×r×2​ 105=100P×r×0.5​ P×r=0.5105×100​=21000​​ Substituting ( P×r=21000P \times r = 21000P×r=21000​ ) into the first equation: ​4620=P+21000×2100 4620=P+420 P=4620−420=42004620 = P + \frac{21000 \times 2}{100}\\\ \\4620 = P + 420\\ \ \\P = 4620 - 420 = 42004620=P+10021000×2​ 4620=P+420 P=4620−420=4200​​ Now, substituting P = 4200 into P×r=21000 P \times r = 21000 P×r=21000​: ​4200×r=21000 r=210004200=54200 \times r = 21000\\ \ \\r = \frac{21000}{4200} = 54200×r=21000 r=420021000​=5​​ Thus, The annual percentage rate is 5%. Alternate Method: 4725 - 4620 = 105 ( S I of 0.5 year) So for 2 year S I will be = 105 ×2×2=420\times 2 \times 2 = 420×2×2=420 Principal = 4620 - 420 = 4200 From the SI formula Rate = 420×1004200×2=5%\frac{420 \times 100}{4200 \times 2} = 5\%4200×2420×100​=5%​​

questions.question: A table whose marked price ₹300 is sold at two successive discounts of 20% and 10%. If in addition, a discount of 5% is offered on cash payment, then the cash price for the table is:
questions.explanation: Given: Marked price of the table = Rs. 300 Successive discounts: 20% and 10% Additional discount on cash payment: 5% Formula Used: ​Selling Price (SP)=Marked Price (MP)×(1−First Discount%100)×(1−Second Discount%100)×(1−Cash Discount%100)\text{Selling Price (SP)} = \text{Marked Price (MP)} \times \left( 1 - \frac{\text{First Discount\%}}{100} \right) \times \left( 1 - \frac{\text{Second Discount\%}}{100} \right) \times \left( 1 - \frac{\text{Cash Discount\%}}{100} \right)Selling Price (SP)=Marked Price (MP)×(1−100First Discount%​)×(1−100Second Discount%​)×(1−100Cash Discount%​)​​ Solution: According to the question: Cash price for the table = 300×80100×90100×95100=205.20300 \times \frac{80}{100} \times \frac{90}{100} \times \frac{95}{100} = 205.20300×10080​×10090​×10095​=205.20​​

questions.question: Find the relation between x and y such that the point (x, y) is equidistant from (6, 2) and (4, 6).
questions.explanation: Given: Point A = (6, 2) Point B = (4, 6) Point (x, y) is equidistant from both A and B Concept Used: A point is equidistant from two given points if its distance from each of those two points is the same. Formula Used: Distance between two point = (x2−x1)2+(y2−y1)2\sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2}(x2​−x1​)2+(y2​−y1​)2​​​ Solution: Distance from (x, y) to (6, 2) = (x−6)2+(y−2)2 \sqrt{(x - 6)^2 + (y - 2)^2} \\(x−6)2+(y−2)2​​ Distance from (x, y) to (4, 6) =(x−4)2+(y−6)2= \sqrt{(x - 4)^2 + (y - 6)^2} \\=(x−4)2+(y−6)2​​ As distance are equal from both points: ​(x−6)2+(y−2)2=(x−4)2+(y−6)2x2−12x+36+y2−4y+4=x2−8x+16+y2−12y+36−12x−4y+40=−8x−12y+52−4x+8y−12=0−x+2y−3=0=&gtx−2y=3(x - 6)^2 + (y - 2)^2 = (x - 4)^2 + (y - 6)^2 \\x^2 - 12x + 36 + y^2 - 4y + 4 = x^2 - 8x + 16 + y^2 - 12y + 36 \\-12x - 4y + 40 = -8x - 12y + 52 \\-4x + 8y - 12 = 0 \\-x + 2y - 3 = 0 \\\Rightarrow x - 2y = 3(x−6)2+(y−2)2=(x−4)2+(y−6)2x2−12x+36+y2−4y+4=x2−8x+16+y2−12y+36−12x−4y+40=−8x−12y+52−4x+8y−12=0−x+2y−3=0=&gtx−2y=3​​

questions.question: An airplane is in level flight at an altitude of 1.5 km from the ground is observed at an angle of elevation of 60°. After 10 seconds, its angle of elevation is observed to be 30°. The speed of the airplane in km/hr is:
questions.explanation: Given: Altitude of the airplane, h = 1.5 km = 1500 m Initial angle of elevation, θ1=60∘\theta_1 = 60^{\circ}θ1​=60∘​​ Final angle of elevation after 10 seconds, θ2=30∘\theta_2 = 30^{\circ}θ2​=30∘​​ Time taken, t = 10 s Formula Used: ​tan⁡θ=OppositeAdwacent\tan \theta = \frac{\text{Opposite}}{\text{Adwacent}}tanθ=AdwacentOpposite​​​ Speed = DistanceTime \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Time}}TimeDistance​​​ Solution: Let the initial horizontal distance be x and the distance traveled by the plane in 10 seconds be d. From the first position: ​tan⁡60∘=1500x\tan 60^{\circ} = \frac{1500}{x}tan60∘=x1500​​​ ​3=1500x\sqrt{3} = \frac{1500}{x}3​=x1500​​​ x = 15003=5003 \frac{1500}{\sqrt{3}} = 500\sqrt{3}3​1500​=5003​​ m From the second position: ​tan⁡30∘=1500x+d\tan 30^{\circ} = \frac{1500}{x + d}tan30∘=x+d1500​​​ ​13=1500x+d\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} = \frac{1500}{x + d}3​1​=x+d1500​​​ x+d=15003x + d = 1500\sqrt{3}x+d=15003​​ m Now, find the distance traveled d: d = 15003−50031500\sqrt{3} - 500\sqrt{3}15003​−5003​​​ d = 100031000\sqrt{3}10003​​ m Calculate the speed in m/s: Speed = 1000310=1003\frac{1000\sqrt{3}}{10} = 100\sqrt{3}1010003​​=1003​​ m/s Convert the speed to km/hr: Speed = 1003×185 100\sqrt{3} \times \frac{18}{5}1003​×518​​​ = 203×18=360320\sqrt{3} \times 18 = 360\sqrt{3}203​×18=3603​​ km/hr Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: When x is subtracted from each of 55, 50, 23 and 22, the resulting numbers, in this order, are in proportion. What is the fourth proportion of 3, 7 and x?
questions.explanation: Given: (55 − x), (50 − x), (23 − x), (22 − x) are in proportion. Formula Used: If a, b, c and d are in proportion then ab=cd\frac{a}{b} = \frac{c}{d}ba​=dc​ Fourth proportional of a, b and c is given by=b×ca\text{Fourth proportional of a, b and c is given by} = \frac{b \times c}{a}Fourth proportional of a, b and c is given by=ab×c​​​​ Solution: ​55−x50−x=23−x22−x (55−x)(22−x)=(50−x)(23−x) 1210−77x=1150−73x 60=4x x=15 \frac{55 - x}{50 - x} = \frac{23 - x}{22 - x}\\\ \\(55 - x)(22 - x) = (50 - x)(23 - x)\\\ \\1210 - 77x = 1150 - 73x\\\ \\60 = 4x\\\ \\x = 15\\\ \\50−x55−x​=22−x23−x​ (55−x)(22−x)=(50−x)(23−x) 1210−77x=1150−73x 60=4x x=15 ​​ Fourth proportional of 3, 7, 15 = (7 × 15) ÷ 3 = 35

questions.question: A person sells an old laptop for Rs. 18,000, making a profit of 20% after spending Rs. 2,000 on repairs. What was the initial price at which he bought the laptop?
questions.explanation: Given: Selling Price (SP) = 18000 Profit = 20% Repair cost = 2000 Formula Used: Cost Price (CP) = SP1+Profit%100 \frac{\text{SP}}{1 + \frac{\text{Profit}\%}{100}}1+100Profit%​SP​​​ Initial Price = Total CP - Repair cost Solution: Calculate the total Cost Price: CP = 180001+0.20=180001.20\frac{18000}{1 + 0.20} = \frac{18000}{1.20}1+0.2018000​=1.2018000​​​ CP = 15000 The total CP includes the initial purchase price plus repair costs. Initial Price + 2000 = 15000 Initial Price = 15000 - 2000 = 13000 So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Due to decrease in manpower, the production in a factory decreases by 28%. By approximately what percentage should the working hours be increased to restore the original level of production?
questions.explanation: Given: Decrease in production (which corresponds to manpower decrease) = 28% Formula Used: Production = Manpower×WorkingHoursManpower \times Working HoursManpower×WorkingHours​​ Required Percentage Increase = Decrease100−Decrease×100 \frac{Decrease}{100 - Decrease} \times 100100−DecreaseDecrease​×100​​ Solution: Let the initial manpower and working hours be 100 each. Original production = 10000. New manpower = 100 - 28 = 72. To restore original production, New Manpower \times New Working Hours = 10000. 72 \times New Working Hours = 10000 New Working Hours = 1000072\frac{10000}{72} 7210000​​ = 138.88 Increase in working hours = 138.88...−100=38.88...%≈38.89% 138.88... - 100 = 38.88...\% \approx 38.89\%138.88...−100=38.88...%≈38.89%​​ So the correct answer is (c).

questions.question: The marks obtained by 11 students in a mathematics test are as follows: 45, 62, 58, 71, 66, 54, 60, 49, 68, 57, 63. What is the median marks?
questions.explanation: Given Marks of 11 students: 45, 62, 58, 71, 66, 54, 60, 49, 68, 57, 63 Total number of terms (n) = 11 Formula Used Median = Value of the n+12th\frac{n+1}{2}^{ th}2n+1​th​ term in a sorted dataset (for odd n) Solution First, arrange the marks in ascending order: 45, 49, 54, 57, 58, 60, 62, 63, 66, 68, 71 Since n = 11 (an odd number), the median is the 11+12th\frac{11 + 1}{2} ^{th}211+1​th​ term. Median = 6th term. Counting to the 6th position in the sorted list, we find the value is 60. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If a = 7 + 4√3, then evaluate a3+1a3a^3 + \frac{1}{a^3}a3+a31​​
Kiran starts business with ₹55,000 and after 7 months, Zarina woins with Kiran as her partner. After a year, the profit is divided in the ratio 8 : 5. What is Zarina's contribution in the capital?
questions.explanation: ​GivenKiran’s capital=55000Kiran’s time=12Zarina’s time=12−7=5Profit ratio=8:5Formula UsedProfit Ratio=Capital×TimeSolutionKiran’s capital-time=55000×12=660000Let Zarina’s capital=xZarina’s capital-time=5x6600005x=85660000×5=8×5x3300000=40xx=82500Final Answer82500\textbf{Given} \\\text{Kiran's capital} = 55000 \\\text{Kiran's time} = 12 \\\text{Zarina's time} = 12 - 7 = 5 \\\text{Profit ratio} = 8 : 5 \\[6pt]\textbf{Formula Used} \\\text{Profit Ratio} = \text{Capital} \times \text{Time} \\[6pt]\textbf{Solution} \\\text{Kiran's capital-time} = 55000 \times 12 = 660000 \\\text{Let Zarina's capital} = x \\\text{Zarina's capital-time} = 5x \\[6pt]\frac{660000}{5x} = \frac{8}{5} \\660000 \times 5 = 8 \times 5x \\3300000 = 40x \\x = 82500 \\[6pt]\textbf{Final Answer} \\\boxed{82500}GivenKiran’s capital=55000Kiran’s time=12Zarina’s time=12−7=5Profit ratio=8:5Formula UsedProfit Ratio=Capital×TimeSolutionKiran’s capital-time=55000×12=660000Let Zarina’s capital=xZarina’s capital-time=5x5x660000​=58​660000×5=8×5x3300000=40xx=82500Final Answer82500​​​

questions.question: A rectangular sheet of a paper, 15 cm long and 10 cm wide, has squares of side 2 cm cut from each of its corners. The sheet is then folded to form a tray of depth 2 cm. Find the volume of this tray.
questions.explanation: Given: Length of sheet = 15 cm Width of sheet = 10 cm Side of square cut from corners = 2 cm Formula Used: Length of tray = Original length - 2 × (cut side) Width of tray = Original width - 2 × (cut side) Volume of cuboid = Length × Width × Height Solution: Length of the tray = 15 - 2 × 2 = 15 - 4 = 11 cm. Width of the tray = 10 - 2 × 2 = 10 - 4 = 6 cm. Height (depth) of the tray = 2 cm. Volume of the tray = 11 × 6 × 2 Volume = 66 × 2 = 132 cm3 Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: The product of two consecutive natural numbers is 306. The greater of the two numbers is:
questions.explanation: Given Product of two consecutive natural numbers = 306 Formula Used Let the consecutive natural numbers be x and x + 1 Product = x(x + 1) Solution Set up the quadratic equation based on the given information: x(x + 1) = 306 x2x^2 x2​+ x - 306 = 0 x2x^2 x2​+ 18x - 17x - 306 = 0 x(x + 18) - 17(x + 18) = 0 (x - 17)(x + 18) = 0 So, x = 17 or x = -18 Since the numbers are natural numbers, x cannot be negative. Therefore, x = 17 The two numbers are 17 and 18 The greater number is 18 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Simplify: cos⁡θ(1+sin⁡θ)+(1+sin⁡θ)cos⁡θ.\frac{cos⁡θ}{(1+sin⁡θ )} + \frac{(1+sin⁡θ)}{cos⁡θ }.(1+sin⁡θ)cos⁡θ​+cos⁡θ(1+sin⁡θ)​.​​
questions.explanation: Given Expression :cos⁡θ1+sin⁡θ+1+sin⁡θcos⁡θ: \frac{\cosθ}{1 + \sinθ} + \frac{1 + \sinθ}{\cosθ}:1+sinθcosθ​+cosθ1+sinθ​​​ Formula Used ​sin⁡2θ+cos⁡2θ=1sec⁡θ=1cos⁡θ\sin^2θ + \cos^2θ = 1\\\secθ = \frac{1}{\cosθ}sin2θ+cos2θ=1secθ=cosθ1​​​ Solution Take the lowest common multiple (LCM) of the denominators: Expression = cos⁡2θ+(1+sin⁡θ)2(1+sin⁡θ)cos⁡θ \frac{\cos^2θ + (1 + \sinθ)^2}{(1 + \sinθ)\cosθ}(1+sinθ)cosθcos2θ+(1+sinθ)2​​​ Expand the numerator: ​=cos⁡2θ+1+sin⁡2θ+2sin⁡θ(1+sin⁡θ)cos⁡θ= \frac{\cos^2θ + 1 + \sin^2θ + 2\sinθ}{(1 + \sinθ)\cosθ}=(1+sinθ)cosθcos2θ+1+sin2θ+2sinθ​​​ Use the identity sin⁡2θ+cos⁡2θ=1:\sin^2θ + \cos^2θ = 1:sin2θ+cos2θ=1:​​ ​=1+1+2sin⁡θ(1+sin⁡θ)cos⁡θ =2+2sin⁡θ(1+sin⁡θ)cos⁡θ =2(1+sin⁡θ)(1+sin⁡θ)cos⁡θ =2cos⁡θ=2sec⁡θ= \frac{1 + 1 + 2\sinθ}{(1 + \sinθ)\cosθ}\\\ \\= \frac{2 + 2\sinθ}{(1 + \sinθ)\cosθ}\\\ \\= \frac{2(1 + \sinθ)}{(1 + \sinθ)\cosθ}\\\ \\= \frac{2}{\cosθ} = 2\secθ=(1+sinθ)cosθ1+1+2sinθ​ =(1+sinθ)cosθ2+2sinθ​ =(1+sinθ)cosθ2(1+sinθ)​ =cosθ2​=2secθ​​ Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: One side of a right-angled triangle is twice the other and the hypotenuse is 15 cm. Find the area (in cm²) of the triangle.
questions.explanation: Given: One side is twice the other. Hypotenuse = 15 cm. Formula Used: Pythagoras theorem: Base2 + Perpendicular2 = Hypotenuse2 Area of a right-angled triangle = 12×Base×Perpendicular \frac{1}{2} \times \text{Base} \times \text{Perpendicular}21​×Base×Perpendicular​​ Solution: Let one side be x and the other side be 2x. Using Pythagoras theorem: ​x2+(2x)2=152 x2+4x2=225 5x2=225 x2=45x^2 + (2x)^2 = 15^2 \\ \ \\x^2 + 4x^2 = 225 \\ \ \ \\5x^2 = 225 \\ \ \\x^2 = 45x2+(2x)2=152 x2+4x2=225 5x2=225 x2=45​​ Area of the right-angled triangle = 12×x×2x \frac{1}{2} \times x \times 2x21​×x×2x​​ Area = x2x^2x2​​ Substitute the value of x2x^2x2​: Area = 45 cm². Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Sooraw is three times as old as his son Arwun, After 8 years, he would be two and a half times of Arwun's age. After further 8 years, he would be times Arwun's age.
The number of diagonals of an n-sided regular polygon is 665. What is the value of ‘n’?
questions.explanation: Given Number of diagonals = 665 Formula Used Number of diagonals of an n-sided regular polygon = n(n−3)2 \frac{n(n - 3)}{2}2n(n−3)​​​ Solution ​n(n−3)2=665n(n−3)=1330n2−3n−1330=0\frac{n(n - 3)}{2} = 665 \\n(n - 3) = 1330\\n^2 - 3n - 1330 = 0\\2n(n−3)​=665n(n−3)=1330n2−3n−1330=0​​ Factorizing the quadratic equation: ​n2−38n+35n−1330=0n(n−38)+35(n−38)=0(n−38)(n+35)=0n^2 - 38n + 35n - 1330 = 0\\n(n - 38) + 35(n - 38) = 0\\(n - 38)(n + 35) = 0\\n2−38n+35n−1330=0n(n−38)+35(n−38)=0(n−38)(n+35)=0​​ Since the number of sides cannot be negative, n = 38. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A bag contains 3 red, 5 blue, and 2 green balls. Two balls are drawn one after another without replacement. Find the probability that both balls are blue.
questions.explanation: ​Given: Red Balls=3,Blue Balls=5,Green Balls=2 Total Balls=3+5+2=10 Event: Two balls drawn without replacement, both are Blue. Solution: Probability of 1st ball being Blue: P(B1)=510=12 After 1st Blue is drawn, remaining Total = 9, remaining Blue = 4. Probability of 2nd ball being Blue: P(B2∣B1)=49 Combined Probability: P(Both Blue)=P(B1)×P(B2∣B1)=12×49 =418=29 Final Answer 29\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Red Balls} = 3, \quad \text{Blue Balls} = 5, \quad \text{Green Balls} = 2 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Total Balls} = 3 + 5 + 2 = 10 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Event:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Two balls drawn without replacement, both are Blue.} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Probability of 1st ball being Blue:} \\ \space \, \\ P(B_1) = \frac{5}{10} = \frac{1}{2} \\ \space \, \\ \text{After 1st Blue is drawn, remaining Total = 9, remaining Blue = 4.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Probability of 2nd ball being Blue:} \\ \space \, \\ P(B_2 | B_1) = \frac{4}{9} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Combined Probability:} \\ \space \, \\ P(\text{Both Blue}) = P(B_1) \times P(B_2 | B_1) = \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{4}{9} \\ \space \, \\ = \frac{4}{18} = \frac{2}{9} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ \frac{2}{9}Given: Red Balls=3,Blue Balls=5,Green Balls=2 Total Balls=3+5+2=10 Event: Two balls drawn without replacement, both are Blue. Solution: Probability of 1st ball being Blue: P(B1​)=105​=21​ After 1st Blue is drawn, remaining Total = 9, remaining Blue = 4. Probability of 2nd ball being Blue: P(B2​∣B1​)=94​ Combined Probability: P(Both Blue)=P(B1​)×P(B2​∣B1​)=21​×94​ =184​=92​ Final Answer 92​​​

questions.question: Veer has Rs. 1632 with him. He divided it amongst his sons Nitin and Pravin and asked them to invest it at 4% rate of interest compounded annually. It was seen that Nitin and Pravin got same amount after 12 and 13 years respectively. How much (in Rs. ) did Veer give to Nitin?
questions.explanation: Given: Total amount = Rs. 1632 Nitin’s investment duration = 12 years Pravin’s investment duration = 13 years Rate of interest = 4% p.a. (compounded annually) Amounts received by both are equal Formula Used: Compound Interest based Final Amount: A = P(1+R100)TP \left(1 + \frac{R}{100} \right)^TP(1+100R​)T​​ Solution: Let Nitin’s principal = P1, Pravin’s principal = P2 Now, ​P1(1+4100)12=P2(1+4100)13 P1(1.04)12=P2(1.04)13 P1=1.04×P2P1\left(1 + \frac{4}{100}\right)^{12} = P2\left(1 + \frac{4}{100}\right)^{13}\\ \ \\ P1 (1.04)^{12} = P2 (1.04)^{13} \\ \ \\P1 = 1.04 \times P2P1(1+1004​)12=P2(1+1004​)13 P1(1.04)12=P2(1.04)13 P1=1.04×P2​​ Also, P1 + P2 = 1632 Substituting P1 = 1.04 × P2 into the total: 1.04P2 + P2 = 1632 2.04P2 = 1632 P2 = 16322.04\frac{1632}{2.04}2.041632​​ = 800 Nitin's principal = 1632 - 800 = ₹832 Exam Hall Method: ​

questions.question: Solve the following system of linear equations: u+v+w=2 6u+4v+5w=31 5u+2v+2w=13
questions.explanation: Given Equation 1: u + v + w = 2 Equation 2: 6u + 4v + 5w = 31 Equation 3: 5u + 2v + 2w = 13 Formula Used Method of algebraic substitution for solving linear equations. Solution From Equation 1, express v + w in terms of u: v + w = 2 - u In Equation 3, factor out the 2 from the v and w terms: 5u + 2(v + w) = 13 Substitute (v + w) with (2 - u): 5u + 2(2 - u) = 13 5u + 4 - 2u = 13 3u = 9 u = 3 Now substitute u = 3 back into Equation 1: 3 + v + w = 2 v + w = -1 => w = -1 - v Substitute u = 3 and w = -1 - v into Equation 2: 6(3) + 4v + 5(-1 - v) = 31 18 + 4v - 5 - 5v = 31 13 - v = 31 -v = 18 v = -18 Finally, find w: w = -1 - (-18) = -1 + 18 = 17. The solution is u = 3, v = -18, w = 17. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: One-third of a certain wourney, is covered at a speed of 25 km/h one-fourth of the wourney is covered at a speed of 30 km/h and the rest of the wourney is covered at 50 km/h. The average speed (in km/h) of the whole wourney is:
questions.explanation: Given: One-third of wourney covered at 25km/hr One-fourth of wourney covered at 30km/hr Rest of wourney covered at 50km/hr Formula Used: Time =DistanceSpeed= \frac{Distance}{Speed}=SpeedDistance​​​ Solution: Let the total distance be x One-third of wourney =13of x=x3 \frac{1}{3} of\ x = \frac{x}{3}31​of x=3x​​​ Time taken to cover the distance at 25km/hr =x325=x75hr \frac{\frac{x}{3}}{25} =\frac{x}{75}hr253x​​=75x​hr​​ One-fourth of wourney =14of x=x4= \frac{1}{4} of\ x = \frac{x}{4}=41​of x=4x​​​ Time taken to cover the distance at 30km/hr =x430=x120hr \frac{\frac{x}{4}}{30} =\frac{x}{120}hr304x​​=120x​hr​​ Rest of wourney =x−x3−x4=12x−4x−3x12=5x12=x- \frac{x}{3}- \frac{x}{4} = \frac{12x -4x-3x}{12}= \frac{5x}{12}=x−3x​−4x​=1212x−4x−3x​=125x​​​ Time taken to cover the distance at 50km/hr = 5x1250=x120hr\frac{\frac{5x}{12}}{50} =\frac{x}{120}hr50125x​​=120x​hr​​ Total time =x75+x120+x120=8x+5x+5x600=18x600=3x100= \frac{x}{75} +\frac{x}{120} + \frac{x}{120} = \frac{8x+5x+5x}{600}=\frac{18x}{600} =\frac{3x}{100}=75x​+120x​+120x​=6008x+5x+5x​=60018x​=1003x​​​ Average Speed =Total DistanceTotal Time=x3x100=1003=3313km/hr \frac{Total\ Distance}{Total \ Time} =\frac{x}{\frac{3x}{100}} = \frac{100}{3} =33\frac{1}{3}km/hrTotal TimeTotal Distance​=1003x​x​=3100​=3331​km/hr​​

questions.question: A batsman had a certain average for 15 innings. In the sixteenth inning, he scored 156 runs, thereby increasing his average by 8 runs. What is his new average?
questions.explanation: Given Innings before = 15 Runs in 16th inning = 156 Average increase = 8 Formula Used Total Runs = Average×Number of Innings\text{Average} \times \text{Number of Innings}Average×Number of Innings​​ Solution Let the initial average be x. Total runs after 15 innings = 15x. New average after 16 innings = x + 8. Equation: 15x + 156 = 16(x + 8) 15x + 156 = 16x + 128 x = 156 - 128 = 28. New average = x + 8 = 28 + 8 = 36. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Three numbers are in the ratio 48:1116:1821\frac{4}{8} : \frac{11}{16} : \frac{18}{21}84​:1611​:2118​. If the difference between the largest and the smallest numbers is 40, find the largest number.
questions.explanation: Given Ratio of three numbers = 48:1116:1821\frac{4}{8} : \frac{11}{16} : \frac{18}{21}84​:1611​:2118​​​ Difference between the largest and the smallest numbers = 40 Solution First, simplify the given fractions: ​48=12\frac{4}{8} = \frac{1}{2}84​=21​​​ 1116\frac{11}{16}1611​​ remains the same. ​1821=67\frac{18}{21} = \frac{6}{7}2118​=76​​​ The new ratio is 12:1116:67.\frac{1}{2} : \frac{11}{16} : \frac{6}{7}.21​:1611​:76​.​​ To clear the denominators, find the LCM of 2, 16, and 7, which is 112. Multiply each fraction by 112: First part = 12×112=56\frac{1}{2} \times 112 = 5621​×112=56​​ Second part =1116×112=77 \frac{11}{16} \times 112 = 771611​×112=77​​ Third part = 67×112=96\frac{6}{7} \times 112 = 9676​×112=96​​ So, the numbers are in the ratio 56 : 77 : 96. Let the numbers be 56x, 77x, and 96x. The difference between the largest and smallest numbers is: 96x - 56x = 40 40x = 40 =&gt \Rightarrow=&gt​ x = 1. The largest number is 96x = 96× \times ×​1 = 96. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)
